newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news since June 6. Stock printed a fresh 52-week high intraday on June 9 ($67.82) on no new catalyst; last fundamentals were the late-April Q1 beat ($139M sales +11% y/y, EPS $0.62 vs $0.52 est., FY26 guide $2.35-$2.45). Next scheduled item is a presentation at the East Coast IDEAS Investor Conference in New York on June 11 (9:55 AM EDT webcast). Small $0.04 dividend payable July 24 (record June 26) declared mid-May.

sources.label: Markets Daily - CTS sets new 12-month high (Jun 9, 2026)
StockTitan - CTS schedules June 11 webcast from East Coast IDEAS conference
GlobeNewswire - CTS to present at 16th Annual East Coast IDEAS Investor Conference
SEC - CTS Corp Q1 2026 8-K exhibit (earnings release)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 2 per yesterday's verdict: today's +0.54% drift and a no-news 52-week high don't change the setup, but the small negative delta flags continued rerating creep (+49.5% YTD) without fresh fundamental evidence, which slowly erodes residual alpha on this crowding-sensitive list.
verdicts.bull: Direct current-sensing and data-center power-sensing exposure at $1.8B cap remains genuinely under-covered; Q1 diversified end-market sales +18% confirms the AI-adjacent mix shift, and the June 11 IDEAS conference could surface new data-center detail.
verdicts.neutral: No new contracts, guidance, or analyst moves since June 6; the stock grinds to new highs on flow, leaving the thesis intact but the entry less asymmetric than when the rank was set.
verdicts.bear: +49.5% YTD and a fresh 52-week high without new evidence means much of the hidden-gem rerating has already happened; transportation (legacy auto) is still the bulk of revenue and growing only ~3%, capping the AI narrative.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 11 East Coast IDEAS investor presentation - any quantification of data-center/AI power-sensing pipeline or design wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression if the small-cap AI-passives trade unwinds; the no-news new-high tape is the classic post-rerating chase this list penalizes.

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 12: the latent-node pathway (edge sensing, thermal monitoring, industrial automation) has no new evidence for or against since June 6, and a +0.54% day is pure drift on a centrality-style screen that is less sensitive to crowding than the alpha list.
verdicts.bull: Q1 diversified sales +18% y/y supports the latent industrial/edge-sensing pathway, and the company's sensors/filters portfolio keeps optionality into thermal monitoring as edge-AI deployments scale.
verdicts.neutral: Nothing since June 6 alters the strict-screen evidence level; node centrality is unchanged and the June 11 conference is informational, not a contract event.
verdicts.bear: The pathway remains inferential rather than contracted - no disclosed edge-AI or thermal-monitoring design wins - and a 49.5% YTD move prices in a pathway that hasn't yet produced confirming evidence.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosure of concrete edge-sensing/thermal-monitoring or data-center design wins, possibly at the June 11 investor presentation or Q2 results in late July.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Pathway never converts from plausible to evidenced, leaving the stock exposed as an over-extended industrial sensor name rather than an AI node.
